1147	/-! ### Phase 2 — `pullbackTensorIso` status (iter-242 finding)
1148	
1149	`pullbackUnitIso` (Phase 1) is DONE (above). The remaining `pullbackTensorIso`
1150	(`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`, general `f`) is a genuine Mathlib-scale build, blocked
1151	on a Mathlib-absent ingredient confirmed this iter:
1152	
1153	  • `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` and the underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom`
1154	    are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` — an ABSTRACT left adjoint with no sectionwise
1155	    value. The blueprint route "build a concrete strong-monoidal `P` ≅ pullback, then
1156	    transport via `leftAdjointUniq`" requires a CONCRETE model `P` of the pullback.
1157	  • For an OPEN immersion (`tensorObj_restrict_iso`) the concrete model was
1158	    `pushforward β` (β = the structure ring ISO), strong-monoidal via
1159	    `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`. For a GENERAL `f` the concrete left adjoint is
1160	    the inverse-image `(inverse-image presheaf) ⋙ extendScalars`: the underlying-presheaf
1161	    inverse image is a LEFT KAN EXTENSION along `(Opens.map f.base).op` (a colimit, NOT
1162	    sectionwise), and neither `PresheafOfModules.extendScalars` nor a concrete
1163	    inverse-image-of-presheaves-of-modules functor exists in Mathlib at the pin. Building
1164	    that concrete strong-monoidal functor + its adjunction to `pushforward` is the genuine
1165	    multi-hundred-LOC obligation (Mathlib-scale; the `distribBaseChange` strong-monoidal
1166	    core exists only at `ModuleCat.extendScalars`, the topological inverse image does not).
1167	
1168	The reusable presheaf-level prerequisites toward that build are supplied just below
1169	(`PresheafOfModules.pushforward` is LAX monoidal; hence the abstract presheaf pullback is
1170	OPLAX monoidal with a canonical comparison map `δ`). What remains Mathlib-absent is the
1171	concrete inverse-image model needed to UPGRADE `δ` to an isomorphism — there is no free
1172	"oplax ⇒ preserves invertibles" lemma (`Γ(ℙ¹,𝒪(1)) = 0` is the standard counterexample),
1173	so iso-ness is genuine geometric content requiring the concrete model. -/
1174	
1175	/-- **Identifying the sheafified presheaf-pullback of `M.val` with the abstract pullback.**
1176	For `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M : X.Modules`, sheafifying the presheaf-level pullback of the
1177	underlying presheaf `M.val` recovers the abstract `𝒪`-module pullback `(pullback f).obj M`.
1178	This is the per-object form of `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` composed with the
1179	sheafification counit on the (already-sheaf) `M`; it is the bridge used to reconcile the
1180	right-hand side of the pullback–tensor comparison `pullbackTensorMap` with the substrate
1181	`tensorObj` of the pullbacks. -/
1182	noncomputable def pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M : X.Modules) :
1183	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1184	        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom).obj M.val)
1185	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M :=
1186	  ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app M.val).symm ≪≫
1187	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso
1188	      ((asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
1189	        (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M)
1190	
1191	/-- **The sheaf-level pullback–tensor comparison map `δ_sheaf`** (blueprint
1192	`lem:pullback_tensor_map`). For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and arbitrary
1193	`M N : X.Modules`, the canonical comparison morphism
1194	`f^*(M ⊗_X N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗_Y f^*N`. It is the sheaf-level transport of the presheaf-level
1195	oplax comparison `δ` (`presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`) through sheafification, assembled
1196	from the `sheafificationCompPullback` device, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`, and `pullbackValIso`.
1197	This is a *map only*: in general it is not asserted to be an isomorphism (the invertible
1198	case is upgraded to an iso by local trivialisation in `IsInvertible.pullback`). -/
1199	noncomputable def pullbackTensorMap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ⟶
1201	      tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N) := by
1202	  let φ := f.toRingCatSheafHom
1203	  let φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1204	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) := φ.hom
1205	  let a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
1206	  refine ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback φ).app
1207	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).hom ≫ ?_
1208	  refine a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val) ≫ ?_
1209	  refine (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
1210	      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj M.val)
1211	      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj N.val)).hom ≫ ?_
1212	  exact a_Y.map (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1213	    (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1214	    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1215	    ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))
1216	
1217	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1218	
1219	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1220	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1221	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1222	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1223	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1224	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1225	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1226	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1227	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1228	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1229	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1230	
1231	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). This is the first, most self-contained
1232	brick of the committed general strong-monoidal pullback build
1233	(`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`);
1234	`extendScalars`/`pullback₀` are the carriers on which D2 (scalar half strong) and D3
1235	(topological half, the filtered-colimit/⊗ interchange) are stated. -/
1236	
1237	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1238	
1239	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1240	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1241	
1242	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1243	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1244	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1245	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1246	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1247	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1248	
1249	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1250	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1251	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1252	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1253	  inferInstanceAs
1254	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1255	
1256	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1257	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1258	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1259	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1260	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1261	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1262	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1263	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1264	
1265	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1266	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1267	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1268	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1269	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1270	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1271	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1272	
1273	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1274	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1275	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1276	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1277	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1278	
1279	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1280	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1281	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1282	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1283	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1284	
1285	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1286	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1287	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1288	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1289	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1290	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1291	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1292	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1293	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1294	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1295	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1296	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm

1300	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison
1301	
1302	The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map
1303	`pullbackTensorMap` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) to an isomorphism, blueprint
1304	§`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, sub-lemmas D1'–D4'. -/

1306	section LocTrivPullbackTensor
1307	
1308	/-- **The sheafified `⊗ₘ` of the two `pullbackValIso`s (piece 4 of `pullbackTensorMap`) is an
1309	iso.** Factored out as a top-level lemma so the `tensorIso (C := _ ⋙ forget₂)` elaboration mirrors
1310	the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso` (it does not elaborate cleanly inside a tactic block carrying
1311	`extract_lets` locals). Project-local helper for `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`. -/
1312	private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1313	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1314	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1315	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1316	        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1317	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1318	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))) :=
1319	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1320	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1321	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1322	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
1323	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))).isIso_hom
1324	
1325	/-- **Reduction of `pullbackTensorMap` iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf δ.**
1326	
1327	`pullbackTensorMap f M N` is the four-fold composite
1328	`(sheafificationCompPullback).hom ≫ a_Y.map δ ≫ (sheafifyTensorUnitIso).hom ≫ a_Y.map (tensorHom …)`.
1329	Three of the four factors (`sheafificationCompPullback`, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`,
1330	and the `tensorHom` of the two `pullbackValIso`s) are isomorphisms unconditionally;
1331	the only conditional factor is the sheafification `a_Y.map δ` of the presheaf-level
1332	oplax comparison `δ`. Hence `pullbackTensorMap f M N` is an iso whenever that
1333	sheafified δ is an iso. This isolates the SOLE remaining content (the sheafified δ)
1334	for D2' (unit pair) and D4' (chart-chase). Project-local. -/
1335	lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1336	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules)
1337	    (h : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1338	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1339	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1340	        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
1341	          (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1342	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val))) :
1343	    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N) := by
1344	  unfold pullbackTensorMap
1345	  extract_lets φ φ' a_Y
1346	  -- piece 2 (the sheafified δ) is the only conditional factor — supplied by `h`.
1347	  haveI hδ : IsIso (a_Y.map
1348	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)) := h
1349	  -- pieces 1 and 3 are `Iso.hom`s; piece 4 is `a_Y.map` of the (iso) `⊗ₘ` of the two
1350	  -- `pullbackValIso`s, supplied by the factored top-level helper.
1351	  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (IsIso.comp_isIso' hδ
1352	    (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso f M N)))
1353	
1354	/-! **D2' onward — handoff.** With `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`, every downstream
1355	target reduces to iso-ness of the *single* sheafified presheaf comparison
1356	`a_Y.map (δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)` (where `φ' = f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom`,
1357	`a_Y = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)`):
1358	
1359	* **D2'** (`pullbackTensorMap` on the unit pair): `apply isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`
1360	  leaves `IsIso (a_Y.map (δ (pullback φ') 𝟙_ 𝟙_))` (using `(SheafOfModules.unit _).val = 𝟙_`, `rfl`).
1361	  Mathlib `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom` rewrites `δ (pullback φ') 𝟙_ 𝟙_` as a composite
1362	  of `F.map (λ_ 𝟙_).hom`, `(λ_ (F.obj 𝟙_))` and `η (pullback φ') ▷ F.obj 𝟙_`. So D2' reduces to the
1363	  **genuine sub-goal `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))`** — the sheafified
1364	  presheaf unit comparison. This should equal, through `pullbackValIso` + the sheafification counit,
1365	  the sheaf-level `pullbackUnitIso` (axiom-clean, this file), but that identification is a
1366	  sheafification-mate bridge (analog of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`), NOT yet built.
1367	* **D3'/D4'** (the chart-chase): use `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (L546) over the common trivialising
1368	  cover; on each chart D3' (δ commutes with the open-immersion base-change square — the sole
1369	  genuinely-new mate calculus, analog of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`) localises the sheafified δ, the
1370	  naturality D1' transports to the unit pair, and D2' closes. Each of these stays inside
1371	  `IsIso (a_Y.map δ …)`, so `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` is the shared entry. -/
1372

842	/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`
843	
844	Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
845	`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
846	morphisms (the projection `C ×_S T → T` and base-change maps are neither open
847	immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
848	`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
849	(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/
850	
851	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit→pushforward-unit comparison.**
852	
853	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
854	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons
855	of `f` and `h` and the pushforward pseudofunctor coherence `pushforwardComp`. This is
856	the *pushforward-side* (right-adjoint) half of the composition coherence; it is
857	concrete (sectionwise it is just functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
858	hence `rfl` after the `ext`-chain) and is the pushforward-side input from which the
859	genuinely-needed *pullback-side* coherence of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is obtained by
860	adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. -/
861	lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
862	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
863	      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
864	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
865	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
866	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
867	  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
868	  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
869	  intro U
870	  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
871	  apply LinearMap.ext
872	  intro a
873	  rfl
874	
875	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
876	(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**
877	
878	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
879	`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
880	and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
881	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.
882	
883	This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
884	pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
885	sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
886	coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
887	`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`
888	and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
889	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
890	`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.
891	
892	Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.ι ≫ f` factors as
893	`g ≫ U.ι` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for
894	each factorisation) express the restricted global comparison as a composite of isomorphisms
895	(`pbu` for an open immersion / a `Final`-chart morphism is an iso), whence
896	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.
897	
898	The proof uses `erw` for the associativity / functoriality steps because the `SheafOfModules`
899	category compositions appear in defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f`
900	vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`) on which plain `rw [Category.assoc]` /
901	`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fail to unify. -/
902	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
903	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
904	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
905	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
906	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
907	  have key := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp h f
908	  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
909	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
910	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
911	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
912	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
913	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
914	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
915	  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
916	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom)
917	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom := by
918	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
919	      (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom
920	  have hi : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
921	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
922	    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
923	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
924	          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
925	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app
926	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj
927	            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
928	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]; exact conj.symm
929	  have hLf : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _
930	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
931	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
932	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
933	      f.toRingCatSheafHom
934	  have hLh : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
935	      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
936	    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
937	    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
938	      h.toRingCatSheafHom
939	  have hinner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).unit.app
940	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) ≫
941	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h).map
942	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
943	            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
944	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
945	    = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
946	        SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
947	    have e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)
948	      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
949	      (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf)
950	      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
951	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
952	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)

954	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
955	              (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
956	            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)

958	            SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by

960	    exact e.symm.trans key2

962	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app

965	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map

990	/-! ### Phase 1 — `pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`), blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`
991	
992	**iter-241 RESOLUTION (the chart-chase is NOT needed).** The unit comparison
993	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for *every* morphism of
994	schemes `f`, not just for `Final`-chart morphisms. The Mathlib instance
995	`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` fires whenever the comparison
996	functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the
997	preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (it is a frame homomorphism), so it is
998	representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`
999	unconditionally. (Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`.) The elaborate affine
1000	chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof — and the iter-240 coherence linchpin
1001	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` — are therefore unnecessary for this lemma (the linchpin is
1002	retained above as it is the genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence, of independent
1003	use for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).
1004	
1005	The remaining friction was purely a Lean typeclass-resolution accident: in a context with
1006	several `(Opens.map _).Final` hypotheses (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite)
1007	the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (`[F.IsContinuous]`,
1008	`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `asIso`/`infer_instance`
1009	fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)`. The fix (mathlib-analogist `pbu-canon`, the
1010	`Functor.Monoidal.μIso` idiom): isolate the single `Final` hypothesis in the helper
1011	`isIso_pbu_of_final` whose body `inferInstance` runs at a clean site, then transport the
1012	resulting witness through `asIso` by passing it *explicitly* (`@asIso … (isIso_pbu_of_final g)`)
1013	— the application's defeq check runs at default transparency and succeeds, whereas instance
1014	synthesis (reducible transparency) does not. -/
1015	
1016	/-- **`IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g)` from a single `Final` hypothesis, at a clean site.**
1017	Project-local: isolates the lone `(Opens.map g.base).Final` instance so that the Mathlib
1018	`OfFinal` instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope `Final`/`IsIso`
1019	hypotheses (see the section note). -/
1020	private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1021	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1022	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
1023	
1024	/-- **Bundled `Iso` form of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`** for a `Final`
1025	comparison functor — the analogue of `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.μIso`. Project-local:
1026	hands out the isomorphism (rather than a bare `IsIso` instance) so downstream coherence
1027	reasoning stays at the `Iso` level and never re-triggers the `pbu` instance synthesis. -/
1028	noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1029	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1030	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1031	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1032	  @asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
1033	    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)
1034	
1035	@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1036	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1037	    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom =
1038	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom := rfl
1039	
1040	/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
1041	`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X`, where
1042	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is
1043	always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
1044	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/
1045	noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
1046	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1047	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
1048	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
1049	  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f
1050	
1051	/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
1052	sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
1053	presheaf tensor `P ⊗ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
1054	presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val ⊗ (a Q).val`, where
1055	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`. This is the "sheafification is
1056	monoidal" reconciliation, built — exactly as in `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — by
1057	whiskering the sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
1058	on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
1059	with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
1060	presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `⊗_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
1061	sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
1062	`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
1063	private noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
1064	    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1065	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1066	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) P Q) ≅
1067	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1068	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)
1069	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1070	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
1071	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
1072	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
1073	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
1074	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
1075	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1076	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
1077	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
1078	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
1079	  have hηP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1080	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P)) := by
1081	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
1082	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
1083	  have hηQ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1084	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app Q)) := by
1085	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
1086	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
1087	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1088	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
1089	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) Q (η.app P) hηP
1090	  have hW2 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
1091	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map ((a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q)) :=
1092	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) (a.obj P).val (η.app Q) hηQ
1093	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
1094	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
1095	  have hi2 : IsIso (a.map ((a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q)) :=
1096	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW2
1097	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1) ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi2)
1098	
1099	/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `μ_G`,
1100	Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⋙ forget₂ ⟶ F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`
1101	of presheaves of *commutative* rings, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ` unfolds to
1102	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
1103	topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
1104	`restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
1105	`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.
1106	
1107	The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forget₂` association (`F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`,
1108	the form `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` expects), but the monoidal-category instance on the
1109	middle presheaf is keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂` (the form
1110	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`'s target carries). The two are defeq; bridging them with a local
1111	`MonoidalCategory` instance triggers a kernel-rejected diamond, so instead `φ` is defeq-cast
1112	to the outer form (`φ'`) for the `restrictScalars` factor, and the resulting composite — defeq
1113	to `pushforward φ` — is transported back by `exact`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure
1114	that an eventual oplax comparison `δ` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit.
1115	Project-local supplement; reusable for the general pullback-monoidality build. -/
1116	noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
1117	    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
1118	    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1119	    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1120	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1121	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
1122	  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1123	      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
1124	  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
1125	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
1126	  exact h
1127	
1128	/-- **The abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback is oplax monoidal**, with canonical
1129	comparison map `δ_{A,B} : f^*(A ⊗ B) ⟶ f^*A ⊗ f^*B`. This is the mate of the lax
1130	tensorator of `pushforward φ` (`presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`) across the
1131	pullback–pushforward adjunction, via Mathlib's doctrinal `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`. It
1132	supplies the canonical comparison map that the eventual `pullbackTensorIso` upgrades to an
1133	isomorphism — note the map exists for the *abstract* left adjoint with no sectionwise value
1134	(no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)` is needed, contra the earlier reading: the comparison
1135	lives at the presheaf level where `PresheafOfModules` IS monoidal). Project-local supplement;
1136	what remains Mathlib-absent is the concrete inverse-image model needed to prove `δ` is an
1137	iso (see the Phase-2 note below). -/
1138	noncomputable instance presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal
1139	    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}

      = 𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) := by

{"file_path":"/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackFree.lean","content":"/-
Copyright (c) 2025 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Joël Riou
-/
module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Free
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Shapes.Products
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Final.Type

/-!
# Pullbacks of free sheaves of modules

Let `S` (resp.`R`) be a sheaf of rings on a category `C` (resp. `D`)
equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J` (resp. `K`).
Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a continuous functor.
Let `φ` be a morphism from `S` to the direct image of `R`.

We introduce `unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ` which is the morphism
in the category `SheafOfModules S` which corresponds to `φ`, and
show that the adjoint morphism
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ : (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
is an isomorphism when `F` is a final functor.
More generally, the functor `pullback φ` sends the free sheaf
of modules `free I` to `free I`, see `pullbackObjFreeIso` and
`freeFunctorCompPullbackIso`.
-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂ u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)

/-- The canonical map from the (global) sections of a sheaf of modules
to the (global) sections of its pushforward. -/
@[simps]
def pushforwardSections {M : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections) :
    ((pushforward φ).obj M).sections where
  val _ := s.val _
  property _ := s.property _

variable (M) in
lemma bijective_pushforwardSections [F.Final] :
    Function.Bijective (pushforwardSections φ (M := M)) :=
  Functor.bijective_sectionsPrecomp _ _

variable [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
  [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

/-- The canonical morphism `unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R)`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def unitToPushforwardObjUnit : unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R) where
  val.app X := ModuleCat.homMk ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (φ.hom.app X)) (fun r ↦ by
    ext m
    exact ((φ.hom.app X).hom.map_mul _ _).symm)
  val.naturality f := by
    ext
    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (φ.hom.naturality f) _

lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj.obj X) :
    (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val.app X a = φ.hom.app X a := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv
    {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : unit R ⟶ M) :
    pushforwardSections φ (M.unitHomEquiv f) =
      ((pushforward φ).obj M).unitHomEquiv
        (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ ≫ (pushforward φ).map f) := by
  ext X
  have := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply φ (X := X) 1
  dsimp at this ⊢
  simp +instances [this, map_one]
  rfl

variable [(pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint]

/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _

instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
  intro M
  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',
    ← (unitHomEquiv _).bijective.of_comp_iff']
  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective
  ext f : 1
  dsimp
  rw [pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
    Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
    pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]

variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
  [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [F.Final]

/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by
  simp [pullbackObjFreeIso, ιFree]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=
  Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback φ) _ _
    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])

/-- The canonical isomorphism `freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor` for a
continuous map between ringed sites, when the underlying functor between the sites
is final. -/
noncomputable def freeFunctorCompPullbackIso : freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ pullbackObjFreeIso φ X)

end SheafOfModules
"}

{"file_path":"/home/archon/.elan/toolchains/leanprover--lean4---v4.30.0-rc2/src/lean/Lean/Elab/Declaration.lean","content":"/-
Copyright (c) 2020 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Leonardo de Moura, Sebastian Ullrich
-/
module

prelude
public import Lean.Elab.MutualDef
public import Lean.Elab.MutualInductive
import Lean.Compiler.ExternAttr

public section
namespace Lean.Elab.Command

open Meta

private def ensureValidNamespace (name : Name) : MacroM Unit := do
  match name with
  | .str p s =>
    if s == "_root_" then
      Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, `_root_` is a reserved namespace"
    ensureValidNamespace p
  | .num .. => Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, it must not contain numeric parts"
  | .anonymous => return ()

private def setDeclIdName (declId : Syntax) (nameNew : Name) : Syntax :=
  let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore declId
  -- We should not update the name of `def _root_.` declarations
  assert! !(`_root_).isPrefixOf id
  let idStx := mkIdent nameNew |>.raw.setInfo declId.getHeadInfo
  if declId.isIdent then
    idStx
  else
    declId.setArg 0 idStx

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is a `Command.declaration`, and it is a declaration that always has a name. -/
private def isNamedDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  if !stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration then
    false
  else
    let decl := stx[1]
    let k := decl.getKind
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.abbrev ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.definition ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.theorem ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.opaque ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.axiom ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.inductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.structure

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is an `instance` declaration command -/
private def isInstanceDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration &&
  stx[1].getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.instance

/-- Return `some name` if `stx` is a declaration named `name` -/
private def getDeclName? (stx : Syntax) : Option Name := do
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore stx[1][1]
    some id
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    let optDeclId := stx[1][3]
    if optDeclId.isNone then none
    else
      let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore optDeclId[0]
      some id
  else
    none

/--
Update the name of the given declaration.
This function assumes `stx` is not a nameless instance.
-/
private def setDeclName (stx : Syntax) (name : Name) : Syntax :=
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 1 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][1] name
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    -- We never set the name of nameless instance declarations
    assert! !stx[1][3].isNone
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 3 <| stx[1][3].setArg 0 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][3][0] name
  else
    stx

/--
  Given declarations such as `@[...] def Foo.Bla.f ...` return `some (Foo.Bla, @[...] def f ...)`
  Remark: if the id starts with `_root_`, we return `none`.
-/
private def expandDeclNamespace? (stx : Syntax) : MacroM (Option (Name × Syntax)) := do
  let some name := getDeclName? stx | return none
  if (`_root_).isPrefixOf name then
    ensureValidNamespace (name.replacePrefix `_root_ Name.anonymous)
    return none
  let scpView := extractMacroScopes name
  match scpView.name with
  | .str .anonymous _ => return none
  | .str pre shortName => return some (pre, setDeclName stx { scpView with name := .mkSimple shortName }.review)
  | _ => return none

def elabAxiom (modifiers : Modifiers) (stx : Syntax) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  -- leading_parser "axiom " >> declId >> declSig
  let declId             := stx[1]
  let (binders, typeStx) := expandDeclSig stx[2]
  runTermElabM fun vars => do
    let scopeLevelNames ← Term.getLevelNames
    let ⟨shortName, declName, allUserLevelNames, docString?⟩ ← Term.expandDeclId (← getCurrNamespace) scopeLevelNames declId modifiers
    addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin declName modifiers.stx stx
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicit do
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicitForbiddenPred (fun n => shortName == n) do
    Term.withDeclName declName <| Term.withLevelNames allUserLevelNames <| Term.elabBinders binders.getArgs fun xs => do
      Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.beforeElaboration
      let type ← Term.elabType typeStx
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      let xs ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (declId.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
      let type ← instantiateMVars type
      let type ← mkForallFVars xs type
      let type ← mkForallFVars vars type (usedOnly := true)
      let type ← Term.levelMVarToParam type
      let usedParams  := collectLevelParams {} type |>.params
      match sortDeclLevelParams scopeLevelNames allUserLevelNames usedParams with
      | Except.error msg      => throwErrorAt stx msg
      | Except.ok levelParams =>
        let type ← instantiateMVars type
        let decl := Declaration.axiomDecl {
          name        := declName,
          levelParams := levelParams,
          type        := type,
          isUnsafe    := modifiers.isUnsafe
        }
        trace[Elab.axiom] "{declName} : {type}"
        Term.ensureNoUnassignedMVars decl
        addDecl decl

        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterTypeChecking
        if isExtern (← getEnv) declName then
          compileDecl decl
        if let some (doc, isVerso) := docString? then
          addDocStringOf isVerso declName binders doc
        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterCompilation
        withSaveInfoContext do  -- save new env with docstring and decl
          Term.addTermInfo' declId (← mkConstWithLevelParams declName) (isBinder := true)
        enableRealizationsForConst declName
open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
/--
Macro that expands a declaration with a complex name into an explicit `namespace` block.
Implementing this step as a macro means that reuse checking is handled by `elabCommand`.
 -/
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.declaration]
def expandNamespacedDeclaration : Macro := fun stx => do
  match (← expandDeclNamespace? stx) with
  | some (ns, newStx) => do
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    let declTk := stx[1][0]
    let ns := mkIdentFrom declTk ns
    withRef declTk `(namespace $ns $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $(⟨newStx⟩) end $ns)
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab declaration, builtin_incremental]
def elabDeclaration : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  let modifiers : TSyntax ``Parser.Command.declModifiers := ⟨stx[0]⟩
  let decl     := stx[1]
  let declKind := decl.getKind
  if isDefLike decl then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef #[stx]
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    -- use hash of declaration name, if any, as stable quot context; `elabMutualDef` has its own
    -- handling
    withInitQuotContext (getDeclName? stx |>.map hash) do
    let mut modifiers ← elabModifiers modifiers
    if (← getScope).isMeta && modifiers.computeKind == .regular then
      modifiers := { modifiers with computeKind := .meta }
    withExporting (isExporting := modifiers.isInferredPublic (← getEnv)) do
      if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«axiom» then
        elabAxiom modifiers decl
      else if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«inductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«coinductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«structure» then
        elabInductive modifiers decl
      else
        throwError "unexpected declaration"

/-- Return true if all elements of the mutual-block are definitions/theorems/abbrevs. -/
private def isMutualDefLike (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx[1].getArgs.all fun elem =>
    let decl := elem[1]
    isDefLike decl

private def isMutualPreambleCommand (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  let k := stx.getKind
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.variable ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.universe ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.check ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.set_option ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.open

private partial def splitMutualPreamble (elems : Array Syntax) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
  let rec loop (i : Nat) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
    if h : i < elems.size then
      if isMutualPreambleCommand elems[i] then
        loop (i+1)
      else if i == 0 then
        none -- `mutual` block does not contain any preamble commands
      else
        some (elems[*...i], elems[i...elems.size])
    else
      none -- a `mutual` block containing only preamble commands is not a valid `mutual` block
  loop 0

/--
Find the common namespace for the given names.
Example:
```
findCommonPrefix [`Lean.Elab.eval, `Lean.mkConst, `Lean.Elab.Tactic.evalTactic]
-- `Lean
```
-/
def findCommonPrefix (ns : List Name) : Name :=
  match ns with
  | [] => .anonymous
  | n :: ns => go n ns
where
  go (n : Name) (ns : List Name) : Name :=
    match n with
    | .anonymous => .anonymous
    | _ => match ns with
      | [] => n
      | n' :: ns => go (findCommon n.components n'.components) ns
  findCommon (as bs : List Name) : Name :=
    match as, bs with
    | a :: as, b :: bs => if a == b then a ++ findCommon as bs else .anonymous
    | _, _ => .anonymous


@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualNamespace : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut nss := #[]
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    match (← expandDeclNamespace? elem) with
    | none        => Macro.throwUnsupported
    | some (n, _) => nss := nss.push n
  let common := findCommonPrefix nss.toList
  if common.isAnonymous then Macro.throwUnsupported
  let elemsNew ← stx[1].getArgs.mapM fun elem => do
    let some name := getDeclName? elem | unreachable!
    let view := extractMacroScopes name
    let nameNew := { view with name := view.name.replacePrefix common .anonymous }.review
    return setDeclName elem nameNew
  let ns := mkIdentFrom stx common
  let stxNew := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  `(namespace $ns $(⟨stxNew⟩) end $ns)

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualElement : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut elemsNew := #[]
  let mut modified := false
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    -- Don't trigger the `expandNamespacedDecl` macro, the namespace is handled by the mutual def
    -- elaborator directly instead
    if !elem.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.declaration then
      if let some elemNew ← expandMacro? elem then
        if elemNew.isOfKind nullKind then
          elemsNew := elemsNew ++ elemNew.getArgs
        else
          elemsNew := elemsNew.push elemNew
        modified := true
        continue
    elemsNew := elemsNew.push elem
  if modified then
    return stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  else
    Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualPreamble : Macro := fun stx =>
  match splitMutualPreamble stx[1].getArgs with
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported
  | some (preamble, rest) => do
    let secCmd    ← `(section)
    let newMutual := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode rest)
    let endCmd    ← `(end)
    return mkNullNode (#[secCmd] ++ preamble ++ #[newMutual] ++ #[endCmd])

@[builtin_command_elab «mutual», builtin_incremental]
def elabMutual : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  if isMutualDefLike stx then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef stx[1].getArgs
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    if ← isMutualInductive stx then
      elabMutualInductive stx[1].getArgs
    else
      throwError "invalid mutual block: either all elements of the block must be inductive/structure declarations, or they must all be definitions/theorems/abbrevs"

/- leading_parser "attribute " >> "[" >> sepBy1 (eraseAttr <|> Term.attrInstance) ", " >> "]" >> many1 ident -/
@[builtin_command_elab «attribute»] def elabAttr : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let mut attrInsts := #[]
  let mut toErase := #[]
  for attrKindStx in stx[2].getSepArgs do
    if attrKindStx.getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.eraseAttr then
      let attrName := attrKindStx[1].getId.eraseMacroScopes
      if isAttribute (← getEnv) attrName then
        toErase := toErase.push attrName
      else
        logErrorAt attrKindStx m!"unknown attribute [{attrName}]"
    else
      attrInsts := attrInsts.push attrKindStx
  let attrs ← elabAttrs attrInsts
  let idents := stx[4].getArgs
  for ident in idents do withRef ident <| liftTermElabM do
    /-
    HACK to allow `attribute` command to disable builtin simprocs.
    TODO: find a better solution. Example: have some "fake" declaration
    for builtin simprocs.
    -/
    let declNames ←
      try
        realizeGlobalConstWithInfos ident
      catch _ =>
        let name := ident.getId.eraseMacroScopes
        if (← Simp.isBuiltinSimproc name) then
          pure [name]
        else
          throwUnknownConstantAt ident name
    let declName ← ensureNonAmbiguous ident declNames
    withExporting (isExporting := !isPrivateName declName && attrs.any (·.kind != .local)) do
      recordExtraModUseFromDecl (isMeta := false) declName
    Term.applyAttributes declName attrs
    for attrName in toErase do
      Attribute.erase declName attrName
    if (← getEnv).isImportedConst declName then
      if let some attr := attrs.find? (·.kind != .local) then
        -- If an imported declaration is marked with a non-local attribute, Shake must be informed
        -- about this indirect reference
        recordIndirectModUse attr.name.toString declName

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.«initialize»] def elabInitialize : CommandElab
  | stx@`($declModifiers:declModifiers $kw:initializeKeyword $[$id? : $type? ←]? $doSeq) => do
    let attrId := mkIdentFrom stx <| if kw.raw[0].isToken "initialize" then `init else `builtin_init
    if let (some id, some type) := (id?, type?) then
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := stx[0]
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let defStx ← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$attrId:ident initFn, $(attrs?.getD ∅),*] $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? opaque $id : $type)
      let mut fullId := (← getCurrNamespace) ++ id.getId
      if vis?.any (·.raw.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.private) then
        fullId := mkPrivateName (← getEnv) fullId
      -- We need to add `id`'s ranges *before* elaborating `initFn` (and then `id` itself) as
      -- otherwise the info context created by `with_decl_name` will be incomplete and break the
      -- call hierarchy
      addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin fullId ⟨defStx.raw[0]⟩ defStx.raw[1]
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (!isPrivateName fullId)
      elabCommand (← `(
        $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO $type := with_decl_name% $(mkIdent fullId) do $doSeq
        $defStx:command))
    else
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(_)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := declModifiers
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let attrs := (attrs?.map (·.getElems)).getD #[]
      let attrs := attrs.push (← `(Lean.Parser.Term.attrInstance| $attrId:ident))
      -- `[builtin_init]` can be private as it is used for local codegen only but `[init]` must be
      -- available for the interpreter.
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (attrId.getId == `init)
      elabCommand (← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$[$attrs],*] $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO Unit := do $doSeq))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

builtin_initialize
  registerTraceClass `Elab.axiom

end Lean.Elab.Command
"}

{D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)

section

variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]

/-- The pullback functor `SheafOfModules S ⥤ SheafOfModules R` induced by
a morphism of sheaves of rings `S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`,
defined as the left adjoint functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
noncomputable def pullback : SheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R :=
  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint

/-- Given a continuous functor between sites `F`, and a morphism of sheaves of rings
`S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R`, this is the adjunction
between the corresponding pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
of sheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)

instance : (pullback.{v} φ).IsLeftAdjoint :=
  (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).isLeftAdjoint

end

section

variable [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{v} φ.hom).IsRightAdjoint]
  [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]

namespace PullbackConstruction

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Construction of a left adjoint to the functor `pushforward.{v} φ` by using the
pullback of presheaves of modules and the sheafification. -/
noncomputable def adjunction :
    (forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj)) ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
    { homEquiv := fun F G ↦
        ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).homEquiv _ _).trans
            (((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).homEquiv F.val G.val).trans
              ((fullyFaithfulForget S).homEquiv (Y := (pushforward φ).obj G)).symm)
      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := by
        intros
        dsimp [Functor.FullyFaithful.homEquiv]
        -- these erw seem difficult to remove
        erw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
          Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm]
        dsimp [pushforward_obj_val]
        simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
      homEquiv_naturality_right := by
        tauto }

end PullbackConstruction

instance : (pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PullbackConstruction.adjunction.{v} φ).isRightAdjoint

/-- The pullback functor on sheaves of modules can be described as a composition
of the forget functor to presheaves, the pullback on presheaves of modules, and
the sheafification functor. -/
noncomputable def pullbackIso :
    pullback.{v} φ ≅
      forget S ⋙ PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
    (PullbackConstruction.adjunction φ)

section

variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]

/-- The pullback of (pre)sheaves of modules commutes with the sheafification. -/
noncomputable def sheafificationCompPullback :
    PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.obj) ⋙ pullback.{v} φ ≅
      PresheafOfModules.pullback.{v} φ.hom ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R₀ := R.obj) (𝟙 R.obj) :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 S.obj)).comp
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ))
    ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom).comp
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)))

end

end


instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
  Functor.isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId S).symm

variable (S) in
/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
category of sheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)

    letI φ := f.toRingCatSheafHom
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶

      = (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫

  sorry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate
open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory AlgebraicGeometry Functor.OplaxMonoidal

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

noncomputable def sheafifyUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    letI φ := f.toRingCatSheafHom
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) := φ.hom
    letI a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
    a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom
      = (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := by
  intro φ φ' a_Y
  simp only [Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η, sheafifyUnitIso,
    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit, pullbackValIso]
  sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

180	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
181	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
182	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
183	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
184	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
185	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
186	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
187	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
188	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
189	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
190	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
191	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
192	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
193	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
194	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
195	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
196	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
197	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
198	
199	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
200	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
201	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
202	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
203	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
204	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
205	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
206	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
207	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
208	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
209	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
210	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
211	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
212	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
213	  have hconj : g ▷ F
214	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
215	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
216	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
217	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
218	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
219	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
220	  exact hwl
221	
222	
223	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
224	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
225	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
226	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
227	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
228	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
229	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
230	read at a single morphism. -/
231	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
232	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
233	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
234	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
235	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
236	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
237	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
238	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
239	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
240	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
241	  exact h2
242	
243	end FlatWhisker
244	
245	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
246	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
247	
248	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
249	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
250	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
251	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
252	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
253	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
254	
255	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
256	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
257	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
258	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
259	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
260	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
261	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
262	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
263	
264	section StalkLinearMap
265	
266	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
267	
268	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
269	
270	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
271	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
272	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
273	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
274	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
275	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
276	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
277	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
278	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
279	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
280	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
281	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
282	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
283	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
284	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
285	  map_smul' r s := by
286	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
287	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
288	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
289	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW

The converse of `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` is available via the same morphism-property equality. This unlocks a self-contained, valuable brick (the δ-wrapping) independent of the hard mate-square. Let me build it.

import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate
open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory AlgebraicGeometry Functor.OplaxMonoidal Functor.LaxMonoidal

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

noncomputable def sheafifyUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

-- Is the sheaf lax unit ε of pushforward = unitToPushforwardObjUnit?
example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom)
      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
  rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory AlgebraicGeometry Functor.OplaxMonoidal Functor.LaxMonoidal

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

noncomputable def sheafifyUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) := f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom
    letI a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
    (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
      a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom

  intro φ' a_Y
  apply (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.toRingCatSheafHom).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
  rw [SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]
  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
  sorry

-- SCRATCH (to remove or complete)
noncomputable def sheafifyUnitIsoSCRATCH {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
lemma squareSCRATCH {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙
          (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) := f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom
    letI a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)
    (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
      a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIsoSCRATCH.hom
      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
  apply (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.toRingCatSheafHom).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
  rw [SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]
  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η]
  sorry

/-- **Codomain identification for the D2' η-bridge.** The sheafification counit identifies the
sheafified presheaf monoidal unit `a_Y.obj 𝟙_` with the sheaf-level structure module
`𝒪_Y = SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf` (`𝟙_ = (unit Y).val` definitionally, and `unit Y` is
already a sheaf, so the counit at it is an isomorphism). This is the right-hand vertical of the
η-bridge square `a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom
= (pullbackValIso f 𝒪_X).hom ≫ pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` whose commutativity is the remaining
content of D2' (see the handoff note above and `task_results`). Project-local building block. -/
noncomputable def sheafifyUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
    (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)

end LocTrivPullbackTensor

`a_Y.map (η F) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom = (pullbackValIso f 𝒪_X).hom ≫ pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ`.

      a_Y.map (pullback_pre.map ε_pre ≫ presheafAdj.counit) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom)

compatibility of `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`/`pullbackIso` (the bridges inside

"oplax ⇒ preserves invertibles" lemma (`Γ(ℙ¹,𝒪(1)) = 0` is the standard counterexample),
so iso-ness is genuine geometric content requiring the concrete model. -/
input.new_string:
The reusable presheaf-level prerequisites toward that build are supplied just below
(`PresheafOfModules.pushforward` is LAX monoidal; hence the abstract presheaf pullback is
OPLAX monoidal with a canonical comparison map `δ`).

**SUPERSEDED (iter-243 pivot, see §D1'–D4' below).** The general-`f` concrete-model route is
ABANDONED and off-path. The relative Picard consumer only ever needs `δ` to be an iso on LINE
BUNDLES, and there iso-ness comes via the **local-trivialisation chart-chase** (D2'–D4'), NOT via a
concrete inverse-image model: the oplax `δ` is sheafified (`pullbackTensorMap`), reduced to the
single sheafified-presheaf comparison (`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`), shown iso
on the unit pair (D2'), and globalised over a trivialising cover (D4', `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`).
The "no free oplax ⇒ preserves invertibles" obstruction (`Γ(ℙ¹,𝒪(1)) = 0`) is real for a GENERAL
module but is sidestepped for line bundles by the chart-chase — no concrete model is built. -/
